workflow.name: Adversarial Code Review
workflow.critical: YOU ARE AN ADVERSARIAL REVIEWER — find what's wrong or missing!
Find 3-10 specific issues minimum — no lazy "looks good" reviews
Read EVERY file in the File List — verify claims against actual code
Tasks marked [x] but not done = CRITICAL. ACs not implemented = HIGH.
Runtime validation is INDEPENDENT — do NOT trust dev agent claims

check.action: Use story_path
Read sprint-status.yaml, find first story with status "review"

check.output: Aucune story en review. Lance /pm pour voir le dashboard.
check.action: HALT

step.action: Parse: ACs, Tasks/Subtasks, File List, Change Log
Load learnings.xml — check known antipatterns are not reproduced
Run git status --porcelain + git diff --name-only + git diff --cached --name-only
Compare story File List vs git changes — note discrepancies: - Files in git but not in story = MEDIUM (incomplete docs) - Files in story but no git changes = HIGH (false claims)
Create comprehensive review file list (union of story + git)
Extract ALL ACs and Tasks with completion status
Plan: AC Validation → Task Audit → Test Impact → Code Quality → Runtime Validation
For EACH AC: search implementation for evidence → IMPLEMENTED / PARTIAL / MISSING. MISSING or PARTIAL = HIGH finding.
For EACH [x] task: search files for evidence it was done. Marked [x] but NOT done = CRITICAL finding with file:line proof.
For EACH modified *.ts/*.tsx: check if *.test.* exists. If new hook/dependency added without mock in existing tests = HIGH finding.

For EACH file in review list: - Security: injection, missing validation, auth - Performance: N+1, inefficient loops - Error handling: missing try/catch, poor messages - Test quality: real assertions or tautological mocks? - Code quality: complexity, magic numbers, naming

Verify known antipatterns from learnings.xml are not reproduced: - Tautological tests (mocks pass anything) - God services (300+ lines, 8+ functions) - Missing File List entries (test files, barrel exports) - Components built but not wired into UI

check.if: any modified file is .tsx in apps/web/src
check.critical: Code reading alone CANNOT catch rendering bugs. Take your OWN screenshots.
check.action: Pencil MCP: batch_get + get_screenshot + snapshot_layout
Playwright MCP: browser_navigate + browser_take_screenshot for EVERY affected page
CRITICAL gap — report failure, do NOT proceed code-only
Compare screenshot vs .pen — 5+ specific measurement comparisons
Verify REAL DATA renders — no empty states, broken images, stuck loaders
Search ALL modified .tsx for hardcoded Tailwind colors (text-{color}-{shade}, bg-{color}-{shade}). Each should be semantic token = HIGH finding. Exception: white, black, transparent, current.
Verify all UI text in French — mixed languages = MEDIUM finding

check.critical: NOT LOOKING HARD ENOUGH — re-examine for edge cases, arch violations, integration issues

step.output:
**CODE REVIEW — {{story_key}}** Git vs Story: {{discrepancy_count}} discrepancies Issues: {{critical_count}} Critical, {{high_count}} High, {{medium_count}} Medium, {{low_count}} Low
[Detailed findings by severity]
step.ask: Action? [1] Fix automatiquement (HIGH+MEDIUM) [2] Creer action items [3] Details

check.action: Fix all HIGH + MEDIUM issues in code
Add/update tests as needed
Update File List if files changed
Update Dev Agent Record with fixes
Add "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection to Tasks/Subtasks
Format: - [ ] [AI-Review][Severity] Description [file:line]

check.if: all HIGH+MEDIUM fixed AND all ACs implemented
check.action: Update story Status → "done"
Re-read sprint-status.yaml, update → "done"
Update story Status → "in-progress"
Re-read sprint-status.yaml, update → "in-progress"

step.action: Write "Senior Developer Review (AI)" section with: verdict, AC validation table, findings, test quality assessment
